sections.title: A: Write one word in each gap
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence:
Dear Gemma,
Hi! How are you? I finally persuaded my mum to take me shopping yesterday. It was great! I tried (1) ...... lots of clothes and I was amazed (2) ...... the choice in all the shops. It reminded me (3) ...... the time we went shopping when you came to stay. We had a lot of fun, didn't we?
Anyway, I found one of those jackets that are (4) ...... fashion at the moment – but I couldn't do it (5) ...... ! I needed a bigger size, but they didn't have any. Oh, well. Maybe I'll have another look (6) ...... the end of this week. I bought some shoes, though – they're similar (7) ...... the ones you've got, the pink ones. I have them (8) ...... at the moment and they look great!
Oh, and you know that old blouse I had? Well, I've changed it (9) ...... a T-shirt! I cut the sleeves (10) ...... and now it'll be perfect for the summer.
I have to go now. Write soon and tell me all your news.
Lots of love,
Olivia
She described the picture she was painting ......
options: A in designing buildings.
B to Martin, but he didn't understand.
C from the exhibition because it was damaged.
D from a winter scene into a spring scene.
E of a horse and it was brilliant.
F on many other painters.
G as modern, but it looked quite old-fashioned to me.
H with his sculptures.

sections.title: C: Complete the second sentence using the word given
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
'I want to learn how to paint,' Janet said. **said**
Janet ...... to learn how to paint.

'We're building a house outside town,' said Mrs Turner. **they**
Mrs Turner ...... a house outside town.

'We're working on our website tomorrow,' Keith said. **day**
Keith said they were working on their website ...... .

'Will you draw me a picture?' Jenny asked. **draw**
Jenny asked ...... a picture.
